titleStmt.title: Keiyō Tei-shi hi, vol. 1-2 (rubbing)
熒陽鄭氏碑 一、二（拓本）

msIdentifier.repository: National Diet Library (国立国会図書館), NDL Digital Collections

msContents.summary: Ink rubbing of a Zheng Daozhao 鄭道昭 (Tei Dōshō) Daoist cliff inscription: Keiyō Tei-shi hi, vol. 1-2. Holding institution: National Diet Library (国立国会図書館), NDL Digital Collections. Access Restrictions: PDM (public domain, full image access). Verified IIIF v2 manifest (sequences/canvases present). 熒陽鄭氏碑 is the standard Japanese title for Zheng Daozhao's 鄭文公碑/鄭羲碑; the editor 日下部東作=日下部鳴鶴 is the Meiji calligrapher who transmitted Zheng Daozhao's style in Japan. This is part 1-2 of a 10-fascicle set (pids 780575-780578).

support.objectType: 拓片 · ink rubbing on paper

origin.origDate: Rubbing-album reproduction, Meiji 14 (1881), 林安之助 [et al.]; collated (校) by 日下部東作 (Kusakabe Meikaku). Original inscription 511 CE (Northern Wei).

bibl.ref: National Diet Library (国立国会図書館), NDL Digital Collections — record

revisionDesc.change: Zheng Daozhao 鄭道昭 (Tei Dōshō) Daoist cliff inscription; harvested from National Diet Library (国立国会図書館), NDL Digital Collections via the Epiwen Japanese-repository workflow. IIIF deep-zoom (CORS-enabled).

div.p: Ink rubbing — see the linked record/manifest.
